title: Precept - Semantic Visual System
description: A composed documentation experiment for the semantic visual system: markdown carries narrative flow, HTML islands carry dense visual proof, and a tiny local builder assembles the result.

sections.title: The Semantic World
sections.summary: Orientation and framing stay markdown-led so the document remains easy to revise without flattening the composition.

sections.title: Reading Model
sections.summary: The document defines how semantic identity, modifiers, overlays, and cross-surface translation work as a single reading grammar.

sections.title: Typography Functions
sections.summary: Typography is a semantic system here: mono governs structure, sans carries reading voice, and italic is reserved for constraint pressure.

sections.title: Semantic Colors
sections.summary: Color is the first read of construct family, while verdicts and comments stay explicitly outside the five authored lanes.

sections.title: Construct Vocabulary
sections.summary: Dense repeated specimens are composed as an island so the gallery can stay structured without forcing the whole page to be handwritten HTML.

sections.title: Surface Translation
sections.summary: The system is only real if authored meaning survives translation into editor, diagram, form, timeline, and documentation surfaces.

sections.title: Documentation Conventions
sections.summary: The documentation layer should reinforce semantic meaning without inventing a second visual language just for explanatory pages.
